overview: Over the 10-year period (2016-2025), PSLE English Paper 1 writing tasks reveal a strong emphasis on peer-to-peer influence and personal growth. In Situational Writing, the dominant communicative move is persuading or encouraging a peer to participate in an activity, appearing four times. Asking for permission from authority figures is the second most common. Formal complaint or apology letters are notably absent. For Continuous Writing, the themes heavily lean towards character-building and emotional experiences, such as gratitude, patience, teamwork, and self-improvement. Action-driven themes like accidents or emergencies are rare, with the focus instead placed on reflective narratives.
prediction: For the next year, a Situational task involving 'Inform / Report an incident to an authority figure' is highly likely, as it has not appeared since 2018. For Continuous Writing, a theme related to 'Teamwork' or 'Surprises and Revelations' is overdue, given they last appeared in 2018 and 2016 respectively.

situationalThemes.name: Persuade / Encourage a peer
situationalThemes.description: Convince a same-age classmate, friend, or relative to take part in an event or competition.

yearsAppeared.note: encourage cousin to participate in art competition
encourage Devi to join your team
persuade Aisha to join the event
persuade Gary to join your group

situationalThemes.name: Ask for permission from an authority figure
situationalThemes.description: Formally request approval from a principal or teacher to conduct an activity.

yearsAppeared.note: ask principal for permission for a performance
ask teacher for permission to make an announcement

situationalThemes.name: Recommend / Nominate someone
situationalThemes.description: Write to an official to suggest someone for an award or recognition based on their good deeds.

yearsAppeared.note: ask manager to consider Paul Tan for Caring Student Award

situationalThemes.name: Inform / Report an incident
situationalThemes.description: Provide a factual account of an event or conflict to a person in charge.

yearsAppeared.note: inform Discipline Master about an incident

situationalThemes.name: Invite a peer
situationalThemes.description: Ask a friend to attend a social outing or event with you.

yearsAppeared.note: invite best friend to watch a movie

situationalThemes.name: Recount / Share an experience
situationalThemes.description: Write an article or notice to share a personal learning journey with peers.

yearsAppeared.note: tell classmates about experience learning a new sport

continuousThemes.name: Gratitude / Appreciation
continuousThemes.description: Stories centered on giving, receiving, or feeling thankful for something or someone.

yearsAppeared.note: gift box, best friends mug, sports jersey
thank you classroom, sunflower, priority seat sign

continuousThemes.name: Surprises and Revelations
continuousThemes.description: Narratives involving hidden information, mysteries, or unexpected discoveries.

yearsAppeared.note: whispering, sealed envelope, padlock

continuousThemes.name: Teamwork / Collaboration
continuousThemes.description: Working together with others to achieve a common goal or overcome a challenge.

yearsAppeared.note: students at computer, children cooking, large trophy

continuousThemes.name: Celebration / Milestones
continuousThemes.description: Joyous occasions, parties, or events marking a special achievement or date.

yearsAppeared.note: children dancing, family photo, invitation card

continuousThemes.name: Loss / Something Missing
continuousThemes.description: Story centered on losing or recovering an item, pet, or person.

yearsAppeared.note: lost phone, information counter, missing cat poster

continuousThemes.name: Promises and Trust
continuousThemes.description: Making, keeping, or breaking a commitment to someone.

yearsAppeared.note: girl writing, wristwatch, hamburger

continuousThemes.name: Endurance / Patience
continuousThemes.description: Experiencing a delay, anticipating an event, or waiting for a long time.

yearsAppeared.note: bored girl, circled calendar date, long queue

continuousThemes.name: Change / Self-Improvement
continuousThemes.description: A turning point where a character improves their habits, attitude, or situation.

yearsAppeared.note: boy jogging, messy bedroom, laptop

continuousThemes.name: First-time Experiences
continuousThemes.description: Stepping out of one's comfort zone to attempt an unfamiliar activity.

yearsAppeared.note: CCA open house banner, making a sandwich, camping picnic
